title: eriocoma atriplicifolia
intro: Eriocoma atriplicifolia, also known as the woolly plains grass, is a plant species belonging to the Poaceae family, characterized by its hairy and woolly leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eriocoma atriplicifolia include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
